Everytown Estimates that gun violence costs Americans $280 million annually, so it's a no-brainer to take that money upfront to try and reduce gun violence in a way that has been proven in Australia. There are important differences, but, y'know, fucking *try*

Allocate $275 billion to a gun buyback program that would pay up to $700 each for the estimated 393 million guns in the US. This might seem like a lot, but that's less than the universal pre-school and community college programs Biden called for. https://t.co/eGML8XmMBa
Installing CS5.5 - 71 minutes left. Yawn. I hope this means I never have to deal with awfulness from Adobe again. Also world peace.
@Federal_flashes respect.

As a concession to the conservatives, I would consider also consider setting up a national organization for gun training and licensing, effectively replacing the NRA with a federal agency. I would encourage all states to write immediate legislation requiring that training.

I would also announce a no-questions-asked generous gun buyback policy / non-police collection facilities and offer a rate that greatly outpays collecting copper wire and offers free harm reduction drug use sites on premises. You could get rich returning guns.

None of these things would stop the epidemic of violence we are seeing- but these types of actions are exactly the type of action that voters want to see- and that politicians like Trump are great on delivering on for their voters - I truly wonder why Dems do nothing

I would then ask my attorney general to announce an investigation into all US gun manufacturers, using the template of the lawsuit that 23 states pursued against Purdue Pharma for the opioid epidemic. I would put them on notice that they will be held accountable for any death

I would also issue a temporary executive order telling the FBI to immediately cease performing the NICS background checks that are mandatory for gun sales in 21 states, effectively pausing the sale of guns for a big portion of the country

if I were a politician, and I'm not, I would sign an executive order that said all domestically manufactured assault weapons created after June 1st would need to be immediately sent to NATO forces in Europe to be stored or distributed as aid to Ukraine

banning certain guns: huge 2A debate
banning gun manufacturers from creating any more guns: could probably be done through a creative executive order
